speaker: GARDINER
text: It’s one o’clock, boy, is ’t not?

speaker: PAGE
text: It hath struck.

speaker: GARDINER
text: These should be hours for necessities,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Not for delights; times to repair our nature

speaker: GARDINER
text: With comforting repose, and not for us

speaker: GARDINER
text: To waste these times.—Good hour of night, Sir Thomas.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Whither so late?

speaker: LOVELL
text: Came you from the King, my lord?

speaker: GARDINER
text: I did, Sir Thomas, and left him at primero

speaker: GARDINER
text: With the Duke of Suffolk.

speaker: LOVELL
text: I must to him too,

speaker: LOVELL
text: Before he go to bed. I’ll take my leave.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Not yet, Sir Thomas Lovell. What’s the matter?

speaker: GARDINER
text: It seems you are in haste. An if there be

speaker: GARDINER
text: No great offense belongs to ’t, give your friend

speaker: GARDINER
text: Some touch of your late business. Affairs that walk,

speaker: GARDINER
text: As they say spirits do, at midnight have

speaker: GARDINER
text: In them a wilder nature than the business

speaker: GARDINER
text: That seeks dispatch by day.

speaker: LOVELL
text: My lord, I love you,

speaker: LOVELL
text: And durst commend a secret to your ear

speaker: LOVELL
text: Much weightier than this work. The Queen’s in labor—

speaker: LOVELL
text: They say in great extremity—and feared

speaker: LOVELL
text: She’ll with the labor end.

speaker: GARDINER
text: The fruit she goes with

speaker: GARDINER
text: I pray for heartily, that it may find

speaker: GARDINER
text: Good time and live; but for the stock, Sir Thomas,

speaker: GARDINER
text: I wish it grubbed up now.

speaker: LOVELL
text: Methinks I could

speaker: LOVELL
text: Cry the amen, and yet my conscience says

speaker: LOVELL
text: She’s a good creature and, sweet lady, does

speaker: LOVELL
text: Deserve our better wishes.

speaker: GARDINER
text: But, sir, sir,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Hear me, Sir Thomas. You’re a gentleman

speaker: GARDINER
text: Of mine own way. I know you wise, religious;

speaker: GARDINER
text: And let me tell you, it will ne’er be well,

speaker: GARDINER
text: ’Twill not, Sir Thomas Lovell, take ’t of me,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Till Cranmer, Cromwell—her two hands—and she

speaker: GARDINER
text: Sleep in their graves.

speaker: LOVELL
text: Now, sir, you speak of two

speaker: LOVELL
text: The most remarked i’ th’ kingdom. As for Cromwell,

speaker: LOVELL
text: Besides that of the Jewel House, is made Master

speaker: LOVELL
text: O’ th’ Rolls and the King’s secretary; further, sir,

speaker: LOVELL
text: Stands in the gap and trade of more preferments,

speaker: LOVELL
text: With which the time will load him. Th’ Archbishop

speaker: LOVELL
text: Is the King’s hand and tongue, and who dare speak

speaker: LOVELL
text: One syllable against him?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Yes, yes, Sir Thomas,

speaker: GARDINER
text: There are that dare, and I myself have ventured

speaker: GARDINER
text: To speak my mind of him. And indeed this day,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Sir—I may tell it you, I think—I have

speaker: GARDINER
text: Incensed the lords o’ th’ Council that he is—

speaker: GARDINER
text: For so I know he is, they know he is—

speaker: GARDINER
text: A most arch heretic, a pestilence

speaker: GARDINER
text: That does infect the land; with which they, moved,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Have broken with the King, who hath so far

speaker: GARDINER
text: Given ear to our complaint, of his great grace

speaker: GARDINER
text: And princely care foreseeing those fell mischiefs

speaker: GARDINER
text: Our reasons laid before him, hath commanded

speaker: GARDINER
text: Tomorrow morning to the Council board

speaker: GARDINER
text: He be convented. He’s a rank weed, Sir Thomas,

speaker: GARDINER
text: And we must root him out. From your affairs

speaker: GARDINER
text: I hinder you too long. Goodnight, Sir Thomas.

speaker: LOVELL
text: Many good nights, my lord. I rest your servant.

speaker: KING
text: Charles, I will play no more tonight.

speaker: KING
text: My mind’s not on ’t; you are too hard for me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Sir, I did never win of you before.

speaker: KING
text: But little, Charles,

speaker: KING
text: Nor shall not when my fancy’s on my play.—

speaker: KING
text: Now, Lovell, from the Queen what is the news?

speaker: LOVELL
text: I could not personally deliver to her

speaker: LOVELL
text: What you commanded me, but by her woman

speaker: LOVELL
text: I sent your message, who returned her thanks

speaker: LOVELL
text: In the great’st humbleness, and desired your Highness

speaker: LOVELL
text: Most heartily to pray for her.

speaker: KING
text: What sayst thou, ha?

speaker: KING
text: To pray for her? What, is she crying out?

speaker: LOVELL
text: So said her woman, and that her suff’rance made

speaker: LOVELL
text: Almost each pang a death.

speaker: KING
text: Alas, good lady!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: God safely quit her of her burden, and

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With gentle travail, to the gladding of

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Your Highness with an heir!

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis midnight, Charles.

speaker: KING
text: Prithee, to bed, and in thy prayers remember

speaker: KING
text: Th’ estate of my poor queen. Leave me alone,

speaker: KING
text: For I must think of that which company

speaker: KING
text: Would not be friendly to.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I wish your Highness

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A quiet night, and my good mistress will

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Remember in my prayers.

speaker: KING
text: Charles, good night.

speaker: KING
text: Well, sir, what follows?

speaker: DENNY
text: Sir, I have brought my lord the Archbishop,

speaker: DENNY
text: As you commanded me.

speaker: KING
text: Ha! Canterbury?

speaker: DENNY
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis true. Where is he, Denny?

speaker: DENNY
text: He attends your Highness’ pleasure.

speaker: KING
text: Bring him to us.

speaker: LOVELL
text: This is about that which the Bishop spake.

speaker: LOVELL
text: I am happily come hither.

speaker: KING
text: Avoid the gallery.

speaker: KING
text: Ha! I have said. Be gone!

speaker: KING
text: What!

speaker: CRANMER
text: I am fearful. Wherefore frowns he thus?

speaker: CRANMER
text: ’Tis his aspect of terror. All’s not well.

speaker: KING
text: How now, my lord? You do desire to know

speaker: KING
text: Wherefore I sent for you.

speaker: CRANMER
text: It is my duty

speaker: CRANMER
text: T’ attend your Highness’ pleasure.

speaker: KING
text: Pray you arise,

speaker: KING
text: My good and gracious Lord of Canterbury.

speaker: KING
text: Come, you and I must walk a turn together.

speaker: KING
text: I have news to tell you. Come, come, give me your hand.

speaker: KING
text: Ah, my good lord, I grieve at what I speak,

speaker: KING
text: And am right sorry to repeat what follows.

speaker: KING
text: I have, and most unwillingly, of late

speaker: KING
text: Heard many grievous—I do say, my lord,

speaker: KING
text: Grievous—complaints of you, which, being considered,

speaker: KING
text: Have moved us and our Council that you shall

speaker: KING
text: This morning come before us, where I know

speaker: KING
text: You cannot with such freedom purge yourself

speaker: KING
text: But that, till further trial in those charges

speaker: KING
text: Which will require your answer, you must take

speaker: KING
text: Your patience to you and be well contented

speaker: KING
text: To make your house our Tower. You a brother of us,

speaker: KING
text: It fits we thus proceed, or else no witness

speaker: KING
text: Would come against you.

speaker: CRANMER
text: I humbly thank your Highness,

speaker: CRANMER
text: And am right glad to catch this good occasion

speaker: CRANMER
text: Most throughly to be winnowed, where my chaff

speaker: CRANMER
text: And corn shall fly asunder. For I know

speaker: CRANMER
text: There’s none stands under more calumnious tongues

speaker: CRANMER
text: Than I myself, poor man.

speaker: KING
text: Stand up, good Canterbury!

speaker: KING
text: Thy truth and thy integrity is rooted

speaker: KING
text: In us, thy friend. Give me thy hand. Stand up.

speaker: KING
text: Prithee, let’s walk. Now by my halidom,

speaker: KING
text: What manner of man are you? My lord, I looked

speaker: KING
text: You would have given me your petition that

speaker: KING
text: I should have ta’en some pains to bring together

speaker: KING
text: Yourself and your accusers and to have heard you

speaker: KING
text: Without endurance further.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Most dread liege,

speaker: CRANMER
text: The good I stand on is my truth and honesty.

speaker: CRANMER
text: If they shall fail, I with mine enemies

speaker: CRANMER
text: Will triumph o’er my person, which I weigh not,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Being of those virtues vacant. I fear nothing

speaker: CRANMER
text: What can be said against me.

speaker: KING
text: Know you not

speaker: KING
text: How your state stands i’ th’ world, with the whole world?

speaker: KING
text: Your enemies are many and not small; their practices

speaker: KING
text: Must bear the same proportion, and not ever

speaker: KING
text: The justice and the truth o’ th’ question carries

speaker: KING
text: The due o’ th’ verdict with it. At what ease

speaker: KING
text: Might corrupt minds procure knaves as corrupt

speaker: KING
text: To swear against you? Such things have been done.

speaker: KING
text: You are potently opposed, and with a malice

speaker: KING
text: Of as great size. Ween you of better luck,

speaker: KING
text: I mean in perjured witness, than your master,

speaker: KING
text: Whose minister you are, whiles here he lived

speaker: KING
text: Upon this naughty earth? Go to, go to.

speaker: KING
text: You take a precipice for no leap of danger

speaker: KING
text: And woo your own destruction.

speaker: CRANMER
text: God and your Majesty

speaker: CRANMER
text: Protect mine innocence, or I fall into

speaker: CRANMER
text: The trap is laid for me.

speaker: KING
text: Be of good cheer.

speaker: KING
text: They shall no more prevail than we give way to.

speaker: KING
text: Keep comfort to you, and this morning see

text: You do appear before them. If they shall chance,

speaker: KING
text: In charging you with matters, to commit you,

speaker: KING
text: The best persuasions to the contrary

speaker: KING
text: Fail not to use, and with what vehemency

text: Th’ occasion shall instruct you. If entreaties

speaker: KING
text: Will render you no remedy, this ring

speaker: KING
text: Deliver them, and your appeal to us

speaker: KING
text: There make before them.

speaker: KING
text: Look, the good man weeps!

speaker: KING
text: He’s honest, on mine honor! God’s blest mother,

speaker: KING
text: I swear he is truehearted, and a soul

speaker: KING
text: None better in my kingdom.—Get you gone,

speaker: KING
text: And do as I have bid you.

speaker: KING
text: He has strangled

speaker: KING
text: His language in his tears.

text: Come back! What mean you?

text: Now by thy looks

speaker: KING
text: I guess thy message. Is the Queen delivered?
